steps.title: Find the price per parfait
steps.content: The 18 yogurt parfaits cost \$70.20 total, so each parfait costs $$\frac{70.20}{18}=3.90$$

steps.title: Write the total cost equation
steps.content: $x$ fruit cups at \$1.60 each cost $1.60x$. $y$ yogurt parfaits at \$3.90 each cost $3.90y$. Their combined cost is \$94.20, so $$1.60x+3.90y=94.20$$

steps.title: Use the given information only
steps.content: No total number of desserts is given, so the equation should only model the total cost, not a separate count.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice D (‘$1.60x + 3.90y = 94.20$’) uses \$1.60 per fruit cup and \$3.90 per yogurt parfait: $$1.60x+3.90y=94.20$$ That confirms D is correct.
